when_to_use: modal jazz over a static Dorian vamp or a pianoless West Coast cool quartet, brushed floating swing, statement-rest-development melody, 55-175 BPM

defaults.feel: brushed swing, floating and gently behind the beat, no hard accents

signature_moves: So What voicing on Dm7: stacked 4ths E-A-D-G capped with a top major 3rd (B)
modal stepwise shift: D Dorian (Dm7) -> Eb Dorian (Ebm7), a half-step up for the bridge
minor stepwise cell: Dm7 (D Dorian) -> Em7 (E Phrygian/Dorian)
pianoless contrapuntal option: drop the chordal instrument, use two independent horn lines (guide tones + low counter-melody) instead of voicings
cadence_options: no functional cadence needed - a modal section simply shifts to the next mode or returns to the top (see arrangement_defaults)
bridge resolution: the half-step-up mode shift resolves by returning to the home Dorian, not by a dominant-tonic cadence
avoid: fast ii-V-I chord changes - harmony here is a static canvas for melodic exploration, not a vehicle to navigate (see cool-modal-jazz-genre.md SS3)
comping a chord on every beat - use interactive/sparse punctuation instead (see groove_profile notes)

hook_archetypes.name: three-note-motif
hook_archetypes.rhythm: a simple 3-note statement, then 1-2 bars of silence, then the motif returns with a rhythmic displacement or one changed final note
hook_archetypes.contour: stays within the mode's characteristic color (e.g. the natural 6th in Dorian)
hook_archetypes.first_appears: Head, stated by the lead horn alone over the bass pedal

hook_archetypes.name: guide-tone-line
hook_archetypes.rhythm: long held notes tracing the mode's guide tones
hook_archetypes.contour: smooth, minimal movement - a line meant to be sung, not run through quickly
hook_archetypes.first_appears: Intro, over the bass pedal point before the full head enters

intro-pedal.note_lengths: very sparse - the bass pedal carries the section almost alone
intro-pedal.contour: n/a or a bare guide-tone fragment from the lead
intro-pedal.placement: brushed and soft, no strong downbeat emphasis

head.note_lengths: short 2-3 note statements followed by 1-2 bars of rest (see cool-modal-jazz-genre.md SS4)
head.contour: unison or simple two-part counterpoint between horns, calm and unhurried
head.placement: close to the grid, expression carried by tone quality not timing

solo.note_lengths: motivic development - repeat and vary a short motif rather than running scales continuously
solo.contour: explores the mode's color (Dorian nat-6, Phrygian b2) at length, not chord-outlining
solo.placement: close to the grid, comping stays sparse and reactive underneath

outro.note_lengths: the head motif repeats, thinning out
outro.contour: fades (morendo), ending on a suspended Harmon-mute note
outro.placement: slows/relaxes further as the piece fades

drum_skeleton.low_energy: brushed swish continuous, feathered kick barely audible, pedal hi-hat soft on 2 and 4
drum_skeleton.mid_energy: brush swish slightly more present, occasional soft ride accent, still no hard hits
drum_skeleton.high_energy: even at the fastest tempo range (140-175 BPM, up-tempo modal/West Coast) the drums stay brushed and unaccented - density increases via contrapuntal horn activity, never via harder drum hits (see cool-modal-jazz-genre.md SS2)

arrangement_defaults.layout_rules: intro is bass pedal point alone under brushed drums, piano enters sporadically with So What voicings; head is unison/counterpoint horn melody over the static mode; bridge shifts the mode a half-step up; solo section explores the mode with sparse interactive comping underneath; outro repeats and fades the head motif to a single suspended mute note

anti_boredom_rules: leave real silence - at least 1-2 bars of rest after every short melodic statement, per the statement-rest-development cycle (see melody_phrasing.head)
harmony stays modal/static within a section - the only sanctioned harmonic 'event' is the half-step mode shift at the bridge, not a chord progression
piano comping (if present) stays sparse/interactive - never a chord on every beat
the three-note-motif hook must return varied by rhythmic displacement or a changed final note, never repeated identically
drums never harden into an accented backbeat, at any tempo or energy level - brushed and floating throughout (see groove_profile)
